Details: RDX is readily oxidized by UV radiation, and although hydrogen peroxide alone has no effect on RDX, in combination with UV irradiation it does perform slightly better than UV alone.  For example, UV alone was able to oxidize approximately 70 percent of the initial RDX concentration in 10 minutes and completely oxidize RDX in 25 minutes. When combined with hydrogen peroxide, 85% of the initial RDX concentration was oxidized in 10 minutes and complete oxidization of RDX occurred in 25 minutes [1714].  Conditions (H2O2 doses, pHs) were atypical of drinking water treatment conditions.
